hypothesis.thesis_statement: Paper 'Replication of Reference-Dependent Preferences and the Risk-Return Trade-Off in the Chinese Market' (arXiv:2505.20608) proposes a signal in the 'behavioral_chips' dimension. Hypothesis: an A-share adaptation predicts the cross-section of forward returns. Test whether the published effect ports to A-shares.
hypothesis.mechanism: Taxonomy dimension: behavioral_chips (FRONTIER_OPEN). Buildable on: chip_distribution(cyq_perf); price_ohlcv(market_daily). Abstract: This study replicates the findings of Wang et al. (2017) on reference-dependent preferences and their impact on the risk-return trade-off in the Chinese stock market, a unique context characterized by high retail investor participation, speculative trading behavior, and regulatory complexities. source.title: Replication of Reference-Dependent Preferences and the Risk-Return Trade-Off in the Chinese Market

pre_registered_concerns.most_likely_failure_mode: [DRAFT] The paper's effect may not survive A-share microstructure (T+1, limit boards, retail flow) or may be subsumed by existing factors after neutralization.
pre_registered_concerns.weakest_assumption: [DRAFT] That the paper's construction maps faithfully to Tushare A-share fields without definitional drift.
pre_registered_concerns.what_would_falsify_this: [DRAFT] Sealed-OOS rank-ICIR below the committed band, or hard rules failing after realistic costs.
pre_registered_concerns.priors_on_cost_sensitivity: [DRAFT] Review turnover at the chosen rebalance.

arxiv_evidence_context_only.abstract: This study replicates the findings of Wang et al. (2017) on reference-dependent preferences and their impact on the risk-return trade-off in the Chinese stock market, a unique context characterized by high retail investor participation, speculative trading behavior, and regulatory complexities. Capital Gains Overhang (CGO), a proxy for unrealized gains or losses, is employed to explore how behavioral biases shape cross-sectional stock returns in an emerging market setting. Utilizing data from 1995 to 2024 and econometric techniques such as Dependent Double Sorting and Fama-MacBeth regressions, this research investigates the interaction between CGO and five risk proxies: Beta, Return Volatili…

todo_before_registering: Read the full paper; extract the EXACT signal construction.
Implement 'arxiv_behavioral_chips_2505_20608' in factor_library mapping it to A-share Tushare/Qlib fields (PIT-safe Ref(...,1)); confirm fields are registered/approved.
Run the SANDBOX screen + size/industry-neutralized MARGINAL-contribution test vs the catalog (marginal IC × low correlation — the house rule), NOT standalone ICIR.
SET expected_effect = YOUR A-share prediction (point + CI + horizon).
CONFIRM the OOS window is unburned, then factor_lifecycle (draft→candidate) → sealed-OOS.
